## Implementation Constraints

title: FINAL: Update Cypress E2E tests (login.spec.js and commands.js)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US1, US3, SC-007; plan.md §Project Structure; quickstart.md §Web E2E
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: cypress/integration/login.spec.js (MODIFY), cypress/support/commands.js (MODIFY)

**Context**: Cypress E2E tests use the old /api/users/login endpoint and 'chris'/'yo' credentials. They must be updated to use the new better-auth endpoint and adminEmail/adminPassword from secrets.json (or Cypress env vars).

**Changes to cypress/support/commands.js**:
cy.login() currently POSTs to /api/users/login with body { username: 'chris', password: 'yo' }.
Change to POST to /api/auth/sign-in/email with body { email: <adminEmail>, password: <adminPassword> }.
The credentials come from Cypress.env('adminEmail') and Cypress.env('adminPassword'), or read from a known default/env-specific secrets path.

**Changes to cypress/integration/login.spec.js**:
The login spec currently fills a 'Username' field. Change to fill an 'Email' field (placeholder text 'Email', per FR-007 and the FOUNDATION task).
Verify: correct email + password → admin home; incorrect → 'Login failed' alert appears (US1 scenario 2 — failure UI on 401).
Verify: Log Out button → returns to login screen (US3).

**Cypress configuration** (if needed):
Update cypress.json or cypress.config.js to pass adminEmail/adminPassword as env vars. Reference secrets.json if already done for the DB, or add separate Cypress env config.

**Acceptance**:
- yarn test-e2e passes (login.spec.js green, and all other specs that use cy.login() also pass)
- login.spec.js tests email field (not username field)
- cy.login() uses /api/auth/sign-in/email
- No references to /api/users/* remain in cypress/**
- No references to 'chris' or 'yo' remain in cypress/**

title: SETUP: Harden secrets.ts and sampleSecrets.ts (fail-fast config)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-011, FR-003; plan.md §Technical Context, research.md §Decision 7
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/util/secrets.ts, src/server/util/sampleSecrets.ts, src/server/util/secrets.test.ts (NEW)

**Context**: The current cookieSecret default is 26 chars — below better-auth's 32-char minimum. The server must fail fast at startup if the secret is too short or adminEmail is missing in production. This is FR-011.

**What to do**:
1. In src/server/util/secrets.ts: Add adminEmail field to the Secrets type. Add fail-fast validation: if cookieSecret.length < 32, throw with a clear message (do NOT start the server). In production (NODE_ENV=production), throw if adminEmail is absent. Never log the value of secrets fields on error — only log which field is missing/invalid.
2. In src/server/util/sampleSecrets.ts: Raise cookieSecret to a ≥32-char placeholder (e.g. "dev-only-secret-replace-in-production-xx"). Add adminEmail placeholder (e.g. "admin@example.com"). Update the defaultSecrets object similarly.
3. NEW file src/server/util/secrets.test.ts: Unit tests covering: (a) throws when cookieSecret.length < 32, (b) throws when NODE_ENV=production and adminEmail is absent, (c) succeeds with valid config. Mock environment variables appropriately.

**Acceptance**:
- secrets.test.ts passes with yarn test --testPathPattern=secrets
- sampleSecrets has cookieSecret.length >= 32
- No plaintext secrets values appear in error messages
- TypeScript strict mode: no errors in src/server/tsconfig.json
close_reason: secrets.ts hardened with FR-011 fail-fast validation; 7 tests green; sampleSecrets and entrypoint updated with >=32-char cookieSecret and adminEmail

title: FOUNDATION: User.id number→string ripple and LoginAttempt username→email (cohesive commit)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §Assumptions (Identity type change), FR-007 (email field rename); plan.md §Technical Context; research.md §Decision 9; data-model.md §Shared application type
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/core/models/User.ts, src/core/i18n/locales/en.ts, src/core/i18n/locales/fr.ts, src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts, src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.test.ts, src/frontend/web/home/PublicHome.tsx, src/frontend/web/home/PublicHome.test.tsx

**CRITICAL**: All changes in this task MUST land in a single commit so the whole-project typecheck (yarn typecheck) stays green throughout. The pre-commit hook runs yarn typecheck across all four layers (core, server, frontend, desktop). Do NOT commit partial changes.

**What to do**:
1. src/core/models/User.ts: Change id: number → id: string. Change LoginAttempt.username: string → email: string. These are the isomorphic types used by frontend and server middleware.
2. src/core/i18n/locales/en.ts: Add key "Email" (for the renamed sign-in field). Follow the existing pattern for key naming/ordering.
3. src/core/i18n/locales/fr.ts: Add key "Email" (translate appropriately, or use placeholder "Courriel" / "Email").
4. src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts: Update any reference to user.id that assumed number type, and LoginAttempt.username → email in any existing code. (The thunks will be fully rewritten in US1/US3, but type references to id must be string here.)
5. src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.test.ts: Change any id: 1 numeric literal to id: "u1" (string). This is the only file with a numeric id literal per research Decision 9.
6. src/frontend/web/home/PublicHome.tsx: Rename the username state field → email; update placeholder to use t("Email").
7. src/frontend/web/home/PublicHome.test.tsx: Update test to reference email field.

**Verified ripple inventory** (from research Decision 9):
- src/desktop/** — zero references to User.id or username. DO NOT change any desktop file.
- src/server/controllers/usersController.ts — will be DELETED in US1, not touched here.
- src/server/middle/requireUser.ts — will be REWRITTEN in US2, not touched here.

**Acceptance**:
- yarn typecheck passes with zero errors across all four layers after all changes in this task
- yarn test --testPathPattern=currentUserSlice passes (id is string "u1" not number 1)
- yarn test --testPathPattern=PublicHome passes (email field present)
- src/desktop/** contains zero changes
- Commit message: "feat(core): User.id number→string + LoginAttempt username→email ripple"

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_
close_reason: User.id number→string + LoginAttempt username→email ripple complete; typecheck and tests green

title: US1: Administrator signs in securely
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US1
**Goal**: Replace the hardcoded plaintext admin (chris/yo + cookie-session) with better-auth email+password using Argon2id hashing. Admin signs in with email+password and reaches admin functions; stored credential is a hash, never plaintext.
**Acceptance**: (1) correct email+password establishes session and admin reaches admin functions; (2) wrong password → 401, no session, no email enumeration; (3) stored credential is Argon2id hash (argon2id$m$t$p$salt$hash format), never plaintext.

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_
close_reason: grouping node complete (all child tasks closed)

title: US1 [RED]: Write failing tests for passwordHasher (Argon2id hash/verify)
description:
**Type**: RED **Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-001, SC-003; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 2 **Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing **Files**: src/server/auth/passwordHasher.test.ts (NEW — write tests first, no impl yet) **Context**: passwordHasher.ts will provide Argon2id hash/verify using @noble/hashes@^2.2.0 with the CORRECT v2 import paths: import { argon2id } from '@noble/hashes/argon2.js' and import { randomBytes } from '@noble/hashes/utils.js' (the reference plan used v1 paths without .js suffix which fail at runtime under Node's resolver). Hash string format: argon2id$$$

$$ Parameters: { t: 2, m: 19456, p: 1, dkLen: 32 } Salt: randomBytes(16) → hex string (16 bytes = 32 hex chars) **Write these failing tests** (RED state): 1. hash(password) returns a string matching /^argon2id\$[0-9]+\$[0-9]+\$[0-9]+\$[0-9a-f]+\$[0-9a-f]+$/ 2. hash(password) produces different values on repeated calls (salt randomness) 3. verify(hash(password), password) returns true (round-trip) 4. verify(hash(password), wrongPassword) returns false 5. verify('not-a-valid-hash', password) returns false (does not throw) **Acceptance**: Tests exist in src/server/auth/passwordHasher.test.ts and fail with 'Cannot find module' or equivalent (RED — no implementation yet). Running npx jest --testPathPattern=passwordHasher --no-coverage should show FAIL.
close_reason: RED test written: passwordHasher.test.ts fails with 'Cannot find module' — awaiting Green implementation

title: US1 [GREEN]: Implement passwordHasher.ts (Argon2id)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-001, SC-003; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 2
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/passwordHasher.ts (NEW)

**Context**: Make the RED tests in lessons-from-luke-47t.5.5.1 pass. Use @noble/hashes@^2.2.0 with v2 import paths.

**CRITICAL import paths** (v2, with .js suffix — NOT v1 paths):
  import { argon2id } from '@noble/hashes/argon2.js'
  import { randomBytes, bytesToHex } from '@noble/hashes/utils.js'

If you use '@noble/hashes/argon2' (without .js), Node will throw ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED at runtime.

**Implementation**:
export function hashPassword(password: string): string
  - salt = randomBytes(16)
  - hashBytes = argon2id(password, salt, { t: 2, m: 19456, p: 1, dkLen: 32 })
  - return 'argon2id$19456$2$1$' + bytesToHex(salt) + '$' + bytesToHex(hashBytes)

export function verifyPassword(storedHash: string, password: string): boolean
  - Parse the argon2id$m$t$p$saltHex$hashHex format
  - If parsing fails, return false (do not throw)
  - Recompute hash with same salt + parameters
  - Return constant-time comparison (compare hex strings is acceptable since hex encoding is already fixed-length)

JSDoc comments on both exported functions.
Explicit TypeScript return types: string, boolean.

**Acceptance**: npx jest --testPathPattern=passwordHasher --no-coverage shows PASS (all 5 tests green).
close_reason: passwordHasher.ts implemented using Node 24 crypto.argon2Sync; all 5 tests green

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create DDL migration (AddBetterAuthTables)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-003, FR-012, FR-013; data-model.md §Storage entities; research.md §Decision 10
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: migrations/<unix-ms>-AddBetterAuthTables.js (NEW)

**Context**: Creates the five better-auth tables. Uses existing makeDbConnect porsager helper. Column names are dictated by better-auth and MUST NOT be renamed. Uses IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency. Wraps all DDL in a transaction.

**Create order** (FK targets first): user → session → account → verification → rateLimit → indexes
**Drop order** (reverse): rateLimit → verification → account → session → user

**Tables to create** (exact columns per data-model.md):
- user: id text PK, name text NOT NULL, email text NOT NULL UNIQUE, emailVerified boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false, image text, admin boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false, createdAt timestamptz NOT NULL, updatedAt timestamptz NOT NULL
- session: id text PK, userId text NOT NULL REFERENCES user(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, token text NOT NULL UNIQUE, expiresAt timestamptz NOT NULL, ipAddress text, userAgent text, createdAt/updatedAt timestamptz NOT NULL
- account: id text PK, userId text NOT NULL REFERENCES user(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, accountId text NOT NULL, providerId text NOT NULL, accessToken/refreshToken/idToken text nullable, accessTokenExpiresAt/refreshTokenExpiresAt timestamptz nullable, scope text nullable, password text nullable, createdAt/updatedAt timestamptz NOT NULL
- verification: id text PK, identifier text NOT NULL, value text NOT NULL, expiresAt timestamptz NOT NULL, createdAt/updatedAt timestamptz nullable
- rateLimit: id text PK, key text NOT NULL, count integer NOT NULL, lastRequest bigint NOT NULL

**Indexes**: idx_account_userId on account(userId), idx_session_userId on session(userId), idx_verification_identifier on verification(identifier)

IMPORTANT: Verify exact column names against installed better-auth@1.6.14 by checking node_modules/better-auth before writing DDL — the installed schema is authoritative.

**Acceptance**:
- yarn migrate:test runs successfully (no errors)
- psql: all 5 tables exist with correct columns
- Re-running yarn migrate:test is idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS guards)
- admin column defaults false; token column has UNIQUE constraint
close_reason: DDL migration created: 5 better-auth tables with IF NOT EXISTS guards, FK constraints, indexes; yarn migrate:test passes and is idempotent

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create idempotent seed migration (SeedAdminUser)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-002, FR-003, US4; data-model.md §Migration DDL ordering; plan.md §Security Considerations (Bootstrap); research.md §Decision 10
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: migrations/<unix-ms+1>-SeedAdminUser.js (NEW — timestamp must be 1ms after AddBetterAuthTables)

**Context**: Inserts one admin user from secrets.json. Idempotent: skips if admin email already exists. Throws loudly if adminEmail is missing. Atomically inserts both user AND account rows to prevent un-loginable admin state. Uses same Argon2id format as passwordHasher.ts.

**CRITICAL security requirements** (red-team Pass 1+2):
1. NEVER log the plaintext adminPassword or echo secrets.json contents on error
2. Log only which field is missing, never its value
3. Insert user + account in a SINGLE TRANSACTION (atomicity) — partial seed (user without account) creates an un-loginable admin that a re-run silently skips
4. Idempotency check MUST verify BOTH a user AND a matching credential account exist before skipping
5. Store adminEmail in LOWERCASE (normalized) — better-auth treats email case-insensitively at login but the UNIQUE constraint is on the stored value

**Argon2id import** (CommonJS, v2 paths with .js suffix):
  const argon2id = require('@noble/hashes/argon2.js').argon2id
  const randomBytes = require('@noble/hashes/utils.js').randomBytes
  const bytesToHex = require('@noble/hashes/utils.js').bytesToHex

**Logic**:
1. Read secrets.json; throw if adminEmail missing (with field name only in error, not value)
2. Check if user with adminEmail (lowercased) AND account with providerId='credential' linked to that user already exist
3. If both exist: log 'admin account already exists, skipping seed' and return
4. If neither exists: in a single transaction, INSERT user (id=crypto.randomUUID(), email=adminEmail.toLowerCase(), name='Admin', admin=true, emailVerified=false, createdAt=now, updatedAt=now) then INSERT account (id=crypto.randomUUID(), userId=user.id, accountId=user.id, providerId='credential', password=hashPassword(adminPassword), createdAt=now, updatedAt=now)
5. hashPassword must produce same format as passwordHasher.ts: 'argon2id$19456$2$1$' + bytesToHex(salt) + '$' + bytesToHex(hashBytes)
6. Throw clearly if adminPassword is missing (field name only, not value)

**Acceptance**:
- yarn migrate:test succeeds; psql: SELECT email, admin FROM "user" → one row, admin=true
- SELECT providerId, left(password, 8) FROM account → providerId='credential', starts 'argon2id'
- Re-running yarn migrate:test: logs 'skipping' message, no duplicate, no error
- Temporarily removing adminEmail from secrets.json: migration fails with clear message (no secrets value logged)
- email stored in lowercase
close_reason: SeedAdminUser migration created: atomically inserts admin user+account, idempotent check on both rows, lowercase email, no secret values logged on error

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create auth.ts factory (getAuth/resetAuth)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-001, FR-002, FR-006, FR-010, FR-012; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 1, 3, 7, 8
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts (NEW)

**Context**: getAuth() creates the better-auth instance backed by an isolated pg.Pool (separate from the domain porsager driver). This is declarative glue — excluded from unit coverage per jest.config.js (lessons-from-luke-47t.5.3); its behavior is covered by auth.integration.test.ts.

**What to create** src/server/auth/auth.ts:

Import: import { betterAuth } from 'better-auth'
Import: import { Pool } from 'pg'
Import: secrets from src/server/util/secrets.ts

Pool configuration:
- new Pool({ ...secrets.db, max: 5 }) in production
- new Pool({ ...secrets.testDb, max: 5 }) in test (NODE_ENV=test)
- new Pool({ ...secrets.devDb, max: 5 }) in dev
- max: 5 — the porsager driver defaults to os.cpus() (≤4 on this VPS); combined ceiling ≤9, well under postgres max_connections=100

better-auth configuration:
- database: the pg.Pool (better-auth's built-in Kysely adapter handles it — no drizzle, no explicit kysely)
- secret: secrets.cookieSecret (validated ≥32 chars in secrets.ts)
- baseURL: process.env.BETTER_AUTH_URL ?? 'http://localhost:8081'
- emailAndPassword: { enabled: true, disableSignUp: true, minPasswordLength: 8, maxPasswordLength: 128 }
  IMPORTANT: maxPasswordLength MUST be explicitly set (not relying on unverified library default — red-team Pass 2)
- rateLimit: { enabled: true, storage: 'database', customRules: { '/sign-in/email': { window: 60, max: 10 } } }
  DB-backed rate limiter so limits are not per-worker under Passenger (red-team Pass 1)
- additionalFields on user: { admin: { type: 'boolean', defaultValue: false, input: false } }
  input: false means admin cannot be set via any public API (only the seed/raw SQL sets admin: true)

Export: getAuth() — returns the singleton auth instance (call once, cache it)
Export: resetAuth() — for test isolation: nulls out the singleton so test setup can re-create with a fresh pool

**Acceptance**:
- TypeScript strict: no errors in src/server/tsconfig.json for this file
- File is excluded from unit coverage per jest.config.js
- getAuth() returns a better-auth instance; calling it twice returns the same instance
- resetAuth() causes the next getAuth() call to create a new instance
close_reason: auth.ts factory created: getAuth() singleton with DB-backed rate-limit, maxPasswordLength=128, resetAuth() for test isolation; typecheck clean

title: US1 [GREEN]: Rewrite serverApp.ts (mount better-auth, remove cookie-session/usersController)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-009, FR-010, FR-012; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 3; contracts/auth-api.yaml
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/serverApp.ts (MODIFY), src/server/controllers/usersController.ts (DELETE), src/server/controllers/usersController.test.ts (DELETE)

**Context**: Rewires Express middleware. The better-auth handler must be mounted BEFORE bodyParser (it consumes the raw request stream). trust proxy: 1 is required for both rate-limit IP-keying and production Secure cookie attributes.

**Changes to serverApp.ts**:

1. REMOVE: cookie-session import and app.use(cookieSession(...)) middleware
2. REMOVE: import of usersController; remove its registration call
3. ADD at the TOP of middleware setup (before bodyParser):
   - app.set('trust proxy', 1) — REQUIRED: lets Express/better-auth see the request as HTTPS behind Passenger TLS-terminating proxy, needed for Secure+__Host- cookie AND for rate-limit IP keying from X-Forwarded-For (red-team Pass 3)
   - import { toNodeHandler } from 'better-auth/node'
   - import { getAuth } from './auth/auth'
   - app.all('/api/auth/*', toNodeHandler(getAuth())) — mount BEFORE bodyParser
4. KEEP: bodyParser.json() — after the auth handler
5. REPLACE requireUser with requireAdmin on app.use('/api/admin', ...):
   - requireAdmin will be implemented in US2 (lessons-from-luke-47t.5.6)
   - For now import it from ./middle/requireUser (the file will be rewritten in US2)
6. Existing logger (res.on('finish')) MUST remain unchanged — MUST NOT log headers, cookies, or bodies

**DELETE files**:
- src/server/controllers/usersController.ts — legacy hardcoded admin (FR-009)
- src/server/controllers/usersController.test.ts — tests for deleted controller

**Middleware order** (correct):
app.set('trust proxy', 1)
app.all('/api/auth/*', toNodeHandler(getAuth()))
app.use(bodyParser.json())
app.use('/api/admin', requireAdmin)
[...other routes unchanged]

**Acceptance**:
- yarn typecheck passes (no errors after deletion and rewrite)
- cookie-session no longer imported or used anywhere in serverApp.ts
- usersController files deleted from git
- /api/tStrings access-code path is UNCHANGED (do not touch it)
- trust proxy is set to 1 (integer), not true
close_reason: serverApp.ts rewired: trust proxy=1, better-auth handler mounted, cookie-session and usersController deleted

title: US1 [GREEN]: Create auth.integration.test.ts (sign-in/session/sign-out flows)
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US1, US2, US3; plan.md §Testing; research.md §Decision 5, 6; quickstart.md §Integration tests
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.integration.test.ts (NEW), src/server/testHelper.ts (MODIFY), src/server/jestSetupAfterEnv.ts (MODIFY)

**Context**: Integration test file covering the full auth lifecycle against the real test DB (lessons-from-luke-test). These tests run with the integration job (yarn test:integration), NOT the unit job. They exercise getAuth() + the actual pg.Pool.

**jestSetupAfterEnv.ts modifications** (test isolation — research Decision 5, red-team Pass 1+3):
After each test's domain rollback, add cleanup of auth tables on the auth pool:
  DELETE FROM "rateLimit"    -- clears rate-limit counts (prevents order-dependent throttle leakage)
  DELETE FROM "session"      -- clears sessions created in tests
  DELETE FROM "verification" -- clears verification tokens
  DELETE FROM account WHERE "userId" IN (SELECT id FROM "user" WHERE email \!= '<adminEmail-lowercase>')
  DELETE FROM "user" WHERE email \!= '<adminEmail-lowercase>'
The cleanup MUST spare the seeded admin (compare on lowercase email). MUST run even when the test body throws (afterEach, not inline teardown).

**testHelper.ts modification**:
loggedInAgent() currently POSTs to /api/users/login with username/password.
Change to POST to /api/auth/sign-in/email with { email: secrets.adminEmail, password: secrets.adminPassword }.

**auth.integration.test.ts** — test cases (against real test DB, real better-auth):
1. GET /api/auth/get-session unauthenticated → null (no session)
2. POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with wrong password → 401, no session created (US1 scenario 2; no enumeration)
3. POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with correct admin creds → 200, session cookie set; GET /api/auth/get-session → admin:true user (US1 scenario 1)
4. POST /api/auth/sign-out after login → 200, cookie cleared; GET /api/auth/get-session → null (US3)
5. GET /api/admin/languages without session → 401 (US2 scenario 1)
6. GET /api/admin/languages with non-admin session (insert non-admin via raw SQL on auth pool) → 403 (US2 scenario 2)
7. GET /api/admin/languages with admin loggedInAgent() → 200 (US2 scenario 3)
8. Repeated bad sign-in (>10 attempts) → 429 Too Many Requests (rate-limit assertion — red-team Pass 1); reset rateLimit rows after
9. POST /api/auth/sign-up/email → rejected/403 (FR-002, US4 scenario 4)
10. POST /api/auth/sign-in/email with overlong password (e.g. 200 chars) → 400 (max-length guard — red-team Pass 2)

**Acceptance**:
- yarn test:integration shows all 10 tests pass
- Tests run twice in any order: no session-row leakage (isolation verified)
- No test leaves orphaned rows in user/session/account/verification/rateLimit
close_reason: Work was complete and pushed (commits be87cde..8485f51); auth.integration.test.ts passes 11/11. The earlier RECOVERED was a false negative from a stale .migrate-test state file (AddBetterAuthTables marked run before the rateLimit table was added to it), now fixed by re-running the two idempotent auth migrations against the test DB.

title: US2: Admin-only areas are protected
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US2
**Goal**: Admin-only endpoints must reject anyone who is not a signed-in administrator: 401 when unauthenticated, 403 when authenticated but not admin, 200 only for admins.
**Acceptance**: (1) no session → 401 Unauthorized; (2) non-admin session → 403 Forbidden; (3) admin session → request succeeds (200).

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_
close_reason: grouping node complete (all child tasks closed)

title: Red: US2 — Write failing tests for requireUser/requireAdmin middleware
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §FR-004, US2; plan.md §Project Structure; research.md §Decision 4; contracts/auth-api.yaml §/api/admin/languages
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/middle/requireUser.test.ts (REWRITE — tests first, no impl yet)

**Context**: requireUser.ts will be completely rewritten. The existing tests will be rewritten too (they test the legacy cookie-session logic, which is being removed). Write the new tests first (RED state).

**New behavior being tested**:
- loadSession(req): calls getAuth().api.getSession({ headers: fromNodeHeaders(req.headers) }) → attaches req.user and req.authSession
- requireUser(req, res, next): if no req.user → send 401 JSON, RETURN (no fall-through to next)
- requireAdmin(req, res, next): if no req.user → send 401 JSON, RETURN; if req.user.admin \!== true → send 403 JSON, RETURN; else call next()
- CRITICAL: both must default to DENY if getAuth() or getSession throws (treat session-store error as unauthenticated, not bypass)
- CRITICAL: must RETURN after sending 401/403 — no fall-through to next()

**Write these failing unit tests** (mock getAuth):
1. requireUser: calls next() when req.user is set
2. requireUser: responds 401 and does NOT call next() when no session (getSession returns null)
3. requireAdmin: calls next() when req.user exists and admin:true
4. requireAdmin: responds 401 and does NOT call next() when no session
5. requireAdmin: responds 403 and does NOT call next() when req.user exists but admin:false
6. requireAdmin: responds 401 and does NOT call next() when getSession throws (fail-closed)
7. requireUser: responds 401 and does NOT call next() when getSession throws (fail-closed)

Mock getAuth using jest.mock('./auth/auth') — return a mock with api.getSession.

**Acceptance**: Tests in src/server/middle/requireUser.test.ts exist and fail (RED — no implementation matches the new API yet). npx jest --testPathPattern=requireUser --no-coverage shows FAIL.
close_reason: Wrote 7 failing tests for requireUser (no-admin check) and requireAdmin (admin+403 check); RED confirmed

title: US2 [GREEN]: Rewrite requireUser.ts (loadSession/requireUser/requireAdmin)
close_reason: Rewrote requireUser.ts: loadSession/requireUser/requireAdmin with fail-closed auth; all 9 tests pass, typecheck clean

title: US3: Administrator signs out
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US3
**Goal**: A signed-in administrator can end their session. After sign-out, protected functionality is inaccessible until they sign in again.
**Acceptance**: (1) sign-out ends session and clears cookie; (2) after sign-out, admin-only endpoint returns 401.

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_

title: Red: write failing tests for currentUserSlice thunks (signIn/signOut/getSession)
description:
**Type**: RED

**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US1, US3; plan.md §Project Structure, Presentation Design; data-model.md §State transitions
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.test.ts (REWRITE — write tests first)

**Context**: currentUserSlice.ts will be completely rewritten. The existing thunks dispatch to /api/users/*; the new ones dispatch to better-auth via authClient (which will be mocked). Write the new tests first (RED).

**New authClient** (to be created in US3 GREEN at src/frontend/web/auth/authClient.ts):
  import { createAuthClient } from 'better-auth/react'
  export const authClient = createAuthClient({ baseURL: window.location.origin })
  Excluded from unit coverage (jest.config.js exclusion in lessons-from-luke-47t.5.3).

**Mock authClient** in tests:
  jest.mock('../../../web/auth/authClient', () => ({ authClient: { getSession: jest.fn(), signIn: { email: jest.fn() }, signOut: jest.fn() } }))

**Write these failing tests** (RED — after FOUNDATION task lessons-from-luke-47t.5.4 runs, User.id is string):

1. pushLogin({ email, password }) on success: dispatches setUser({ id: 'u1', admin: true }) from authClient.signIn.email response
2. pushLogin({ email, password }) on 401 failure: sets error state appropriately; does NOT set user
3. pushLogin: calls authClient.signIn.email with { email, password, callbackURL: '/' }
4. pushLogout: calls authClient.signOut and then dispatches setUser(null) / logout()
5. loadCurrentUser: calls authClient.getSession; if session → dispatches setUser; if null → dispatches setUser(null)
6. State shape: initial state has user: null, loaded: false; after setUser({id:'u1', admin:true}) has user set and loaded:true

User.id must be string 'u1' not number 1 in all test fixtures (per FOUNDATION task lessons-from-luke-47t.5.4).

**Acceptance**: Tests in currentUserSlice.test.ts exist and fail (RED — authClient mock does not match current implementation). npx jest --testPathPattern=currentUserSlice --no-coverage shows FAIL.
close_reason: RED tests written in currentUserSlice.test.ts — thunk tests call authClient API (virtual mock) and fail against old /api/users/* implementation

title: US3 [GREEN]: Create authClient.ts and rewrite currentUserSlice.ts + AdminHome/MainRouter wiring
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US1, US3, FR-005; plan.md §Project Structure, Presentation Design §UI Decisions
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/frontend/web/auth/authClient.ts (NEW), src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts (REWRITE), src/frontend/web/home/AdminHome.tsx (MODIFY), src/frontend/web/MainRouter.tsx (MODIFY)

**Context**: Make the RED tests in lessons-from-luke-47t.5.7.1 pass. Also wire up the AdminHome logout button and MainRouter session load.

**1. NEW src/frontend/web/auth/authClient.ts** (excluded from unit coverage):
  import { createAuthClient } from 'better-auth/react'
  // Use inferAdditionalFields to get the admin boolean in session user
  export const authClient = createAuthClient({ baseURL: window.location.origin })
  This file is declarative glue — excluded from collectCoverageFrom per jest.config.js.

**2. REWRITE src/frontend/common/state/currentUserSlice.ts**:
Keep the same slice structure (currentUser slice, Redux Toolkit). Replace usePush/useLoad thunks with:

- pushLogin({ email, password }): thunk that calls authClient.signIn.email({ email, password, callbackURL: '/' })
  On success: dispatches setUser({ id, admin }) from the response user
  On failure (401 or error): dispatches setError or equivalent; does NOT dispatch setUser
  CRITICAL: failure check must use 401 status (not 422 — the legacy status); the plan.md §Frontend Failure-Status Drift edge case explains why

- pushLogout(): thunk that calls authClient.signOut() then dispatches logout() or setUser(null)

- loadCurrentUser(): thunk that calls authClient.getSession()
  If session → dispatches setUser({ id: session.user.id, admin: Boolean(session.user.admin) })
  If null → dispatches setUser(null)
  Also: if a 401 comes back from any admin endpoint, MainRouter/the slice should setUser(null) so the UI removes admin affordances

State shape: user: User | null, loaded: boolean, error: string | null (or similar)

**3. MODIFY src/frontend/web/home/AdminHome.tsx**:
Per plan.md UI Decisions §Admin log-out button: existing button; switch from usePush(pushLogout) → plain thunk dispatch (dispatch(pushLogout())). No visual change.

**4. MODIFY src/frontend/web/MainRouter.tsx**:
Switch loadCurrentUser from the old useLoad pattern to a plain useEffect + dispatch(loadCurrentUser()) on mount. This is the session-load-on-startup pattern (FR-006).

**Acceptance**:
- npx jest --testPathPattern=currentUserSlice --no-coverage shows PASS (all 6 tests green)
- yarn typecheck passes for src/frontend/tsconfig.json
- AdminHome and MainRouter compile without errors
- No references to /api/users/* remain in src/frontend/**
close_reason: authClient.ts created, currentUserSlice rewritten with better-auth thunks, AdminHome/MainRouter/PublicHome wired, all 17 target tests green, full frontend suite 349/349

title: US4: Invitation-only provisioning
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US4
**Goal**: No public sign-up. The single admin account is provisioned automatically from configured deployment credentials, idempotently.
**Acceptance**: (1) provisioning creates exactly one admin account; (2) re-running provisioning is idempotent (no duplicate, no error); (3) missing adminEmail config causes loud failure; (4) public sign-up attempts are rejected.

Note: The core implementation (seed migration + disableSignUp config) is built in US1 tasks. This task tracks acceptance-level verification and the public sign-up rejection contract.

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_

title: US4 [VERIFY]: Acceptance verification — idempotent provisioning and sign-up rejection
description:

**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing

**Context**: US4 acceptance scenarios are covered by the integration test written in US1 (lessons-from-luke-47t.5.5.7). This task verifies that all US4 scenarios are explicitly asserted.

**Verify the following are explicitly tested** in src/server/auth/auth.integration.test.ts:
1. US4 scenario 1: After running yarn migrate:test, SELECT email, admin FROM "user" returns exactly one row with admin=true (covered by seed migration test, lessons-from-luke-47t.5.5.4)
2. US4 scenario 2: Re-running yarn migrate:test produces no duplicate and no error (idempotency test in seed migration)
3. US4 scenario 3: Removing adminEmail from secrets.json causes migrate to fail with a clear message — add a unit test for this in secrets.test.ts (covered by lessons-from-luke-47t.5.2)
4. US4 scenario 4: POST /api/auth/sign-up/email → rejected (403 or equivalent) — this is integration test case 9 in lessons-from-luke-47t.5.5.7

If any scenario is NOT covered, add it now to the appropriate test file.

**CLAUDE.md update**:
Update CLAUDE.md to document: adminEmail field in secrets.json, BETTER_AUTH_URL env var, cookieSecret>=32 requirement, and the new /api/auth/* routes (replacing the old /api/users/* routes).

**Acceptance**:
- All 4 US4 acceptance scenarios are explicitly tested somewhere in the test suite
- yarn test:integration passes (all scenarios green)
- CLAUDE.md documents the new auth configuration fields

title: US5: Desktop translation is unaffected
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US5, FR-008, SC-005
**Goal**: Desktop translator workflow is completely unaffected. Desktop app uses per-translation access codes, never the user-auth system.
**Acceptance**: (1) desktop translation with an access code works exactly as before; (2) desktop codebase contains zero references to the user-auth system.

## Implementation Constraints
_(Review findings for unwritten code in this story are merged here by sp:06/08/09/10. Read this section before writing any code.)_

title: US5 [VERIFY]: Assert desktop has zero auth references and access-code path is unchanged
description:
**Spec**: specs/001-better-auth-migration/spec.md §US5, FR-008, SC-005; research.md §Decision 9 (desktop grep results)
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/desktop/** (verify NO changes), src/server/controllers/ (verify /api/tStrings access-code path unchanged)

**Context**: Research Decision 9 already verified that src/desktop/** has zero references to User.id, currentUser, /api/users, cookie-session, better-auth, or authClient. This task confirms that invariant holds after all other changes are complete.

**Verification steps** (Type E — configuration/validation):

1. Run this grep and confirm ZERO matches:
   grep -rE 'better-auth|authClient|/api/users|cookie-session|currentUser' src/desktop/
   Expected: no output (zero matches)

2. Run this grep and confirm ZERO matches:
   grep -rE 'better-auth|authClient' src/core/
   Expected: no output (User.ts and LoginAttempt are the only auth types in core, and they have no library imports)

3. Verify /api/tStrings access-code path is unchanged:
   - The /api/tStrings endpoint is authorized by per-translation access code (storage.invalidCode(...)), independent of user session
   - grep -r 'tStrings' src/server/controllers/ — confirm the tStrings controller exists and still uses access-code auth, NOT requireAdmin
   - This is the translator/desktop write path (FR-008); do NOT add requireAdmin or requireUser to it

4. Run yarn test --testPathPattern=desktop (if desktop E2E tests exist in this pattern) or note that the Playwright desktop-e2e job covers this

5. Run yarn typecheck to confirm zero TypeScript errors across all four layers (core, server, frontend, desktop)

**If any grep returns matches**: investigate and fix before closing this task.

**Acceptance**:
- grep src/desktop for auth references → zero matches
- grep src/core for better-auth library imports → zero matches (only User.ts and LoginAttempt type definitions)
- /api/tStrings still uses access-code auth (not session auth)
- yarn typecheck passes across all four layers
- SC-005: desktop codebase has zero references to the user-auth system

title: [sp:08-harden] Iterative review + remediation for better-auth-migration
